sources.gameUse: Best single annual synthesis for capabilities, policy, labor, science, medicine, and AI sovereignty; ideal baseline world-state reference for opening turns
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. possible-dup-of: The 2025 AI Index Report (annual series; BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.10 / HANDOVER §11 'AI Index Report — Stanford HAI, annual').

sources.title: Frontier AI Trends Report
sources.gameUse: Strongest recent public source for task-length trends, cyber/CBRN/autonomy curves, safeguard bypass effort, and open-vs-closed gap narrowing; useful for escalation mechanics
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW]. Related to but distinct from the UK AISI org entry in BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2 (https://www.aisi.gov.uk/).

sources.title: ARC-AGI-2: A New Challenge for Frontier AI Reasoning Systems
sources.authors: François Chollet, Mike Knoop, Gregory Kamradt, Bryan Landers, Henry Pinkard
sources.gameUse: Successor benchmark to ARC-AGI; better for reasoning-frontier calibration
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. possible-dup-of: ARC-AGI benchmark (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.10, see SRC-ARC-AGI).

sources.title: RE-Bench: Evaluating frontier AI R&D capabilities of language model agents against human experts
sources.gameUse: Most gameplay-relevant capability benchmark for AI-accelerates-AI; grounds R&D-automation, recursive-acceleration, and lab-race dynamics
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: MLRC-Bench: Can Language Agents Solve Machine Learning Research Challenges?
sources.gameUse: Counterweight to hype around autonomous AI science; shows harder limits on novel ML-research performance vs. engineering-heavy tasks
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [BALANCE].

sources.title: International AI Safety Report 2026
sources.gameUse: Most authoritative recent multi-expert survey of capabilities, risks, and safeguards; core anchor document
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. possible-dup-of: International AI Safety Report 2025 (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2).

sources.title: International AI Safety Report 2025: Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards and Risk Management
sources.gameUse: Focuses on operational safeguards and risk-management practice rather than abstract risk alone
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. DUPLICATE: identical arXiv URL (2511.19863) to the source already covered by TAKEN id SRC-IAISR-UPDATE-2 (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2 'International AI Safety Report — Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards & Risk Management'). possible-dup-of: International AI Safety Report — Second Key Update: Technical Safeguards & Risk Management — recommend merger drop this entry in favor of SRC-IAISR-UPDATE-2.

sources.title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy
sources.gameUse: 2026 revisions materially affect how lab self-governance should be represented in-game
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. DUPLICATE: identical URL to TAKEN id SRC-ANTHROPIC-RSP (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2 'Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy'). possible-dup-of: Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy — recommend merger drop this entry in favor of SRC-ANTHROPIC-RSP, preserving the 2026-update framing as a version note.

sources.title: Anthropic's Frontier Safety Roadmap
sources.gameUse: Turns safety posture into dated milestones across security, safeguards, alignment, and policy; highly gameable
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW]. Companion page to Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy (SRC-ANTHROPIC-RSP), distinct /roadmap URL, not an exact duplicate.

sources.title: Our updated Preparedness Framework
sources.gameUse: Clarifies tracked vs. research categories, safeguard reports, and competitive-adjustment logic
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. DUPLICATE: identical URL to TAKEN id SRC-OPENAI-PREPAREDNESS (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2 'OpenAI Preparedness Framework (v2)'). possible-dup-of: OpenAI Preparedness Framework (v2) — recommend merger drop this entry in favor of SRC-OPENAI-PREPAREDNESS.

sources.title: Strengthening our Frontier Safety Framework
sources.gameUse: Strong source for harmful-manipulation and misalignment-risk framing, plus tracked capability thresholds
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE], source says '2025, updated 2026'. DUPLICATE: identical URL to TAKEN id SRC-DEEPMIND-FSF (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.2 'Google DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework (v3)'). possible-dup-of: Google DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework (v3) — recommend merger drop this entry in favor of SRC-DEEPMIND-FSF.

sources.title: Agentic Misalignment: How LLMs Could Be Insider Threats
sources.gameUse: Most directly game-relevant recent paper for sabotage / blackmail / insider-threat event chains under high-autonomy deployment
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW]. MUST-USE id per merge instructions.

sources.title: Training Deliberative Monitors for Black-Box Scheming Detection
sources.gameUse: Good for control-stack / monitoring-investment mechanics more concrete than generic alignment spending
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: European AI Office
sources.gameUse: Captures the implementation machinery, enforcement structure, and current supporting instruments (better than generic AI Act explainers)
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE].

sources.title: Existing Industry Practice for the EU AI Act's General-Purpose AI Code of Practice Safety and Security Measures
sources.gameUse: Bridge source between frontier-lab safety frameworks and EU compliance language; grounds regulatory-meets-technical-measures gameplay
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: Bench-2-CoP: Can We Trust Benchmarking for EU AI Compliance?
sources.gameUse: Shows common benchmarks under-measure the systemic risks regulators increasingly care about
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW]. MUST-USE id per merge instructions.

sources.title: International Agreements on AI Safety: Review and Recommendations for a Conditional AI Safety Treaty
sources.authors: Rebecca Scholefield, Samuel Martin, Otto Barten
sources.gameUse: Strong addition for negotiated-slowdown / treaty / verification endings
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: AI Risk-Management Standards Profile for General-Purpose AI and Foundation Models
sources.gameUse: Standards-oriented complement to high-level governance pieces; helpful for audits/controls/assurance mechanics
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: Environmental Burden of United States Data Centers in the Artificial Intelligence Era
sources.gameUse: Local environmental backlash, carbon intensity, and fossil-fuel dependence around data-center expansion
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW]. MUST-USE id per merge instructions (hint text: 'grid stress / datacenter backlash, Guidi/Chen'). Two qualifying gap-audit papers exist: this Guidi et al. paper on environmental burden/backlash, and a companion Chen et al. 2026 paper on regional power-grid siting stress (filed separately as SRC-DATACENTER-SITING-STRESS). Assigned this id to the Guidi paper since Guidi is named first in the hint; if the intended target was the Chen paper instead, swap ids at merge time.

sources.title: Concentrated siting of AI data centers drives regional power-system stress under rising global compute demand
sources.gameUse: Region-specific bottleneck mechanics: not just total TWh, but clustered siting and local grid vulnerability
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [NEW]. See SRC-GRID-DATACENTER note re: must-use-id ambiguity between this paper and Guidi et al. 2024.

sources.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
sources.gameUse: Grounds reasoning-focused competition, open distillation, and lower-cost catch-up
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEWER-THAN-MINE]. Also listed in HANDOVER §11 'US–China & geopolitics' subsection (outside worker C's assigned HANDOVER subsections).

sources.title: Insights into DeepSeek-V3: Scaling Challenges and Reflections on Hardware for AI Architectures
sources.gameUse: Ties model strategy to interconnects, precision formats, and hardware co-design rather than treating compute as a single scalar
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: U.S. Policies Unintentionally Accelerated China's Open AI Ecosystems
sources.gameUse: Strategic counterpoint: export controls can slow frontier access while strengthening open, adaptive Chinese ecosystems
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [BALANCE].

sources.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Uneven geographic and enterprise AI adoption
sources.gameUse: Best recent empirical corrective to instant-economy-wide-transformation narratives; valuable for adoption-friction and uneven-diffusion mechanics
sources.note: gap-audit tag: [BALANCE]. possible-dup-of: The Anthropic Economic Index (BIBLIOGRAPHY-FULL.md sec.6/sec.10 live dashboard) — related initiative but this is a specific dated research report (arXiv), not the live dashboard; merger should decide whether to keep both.

sources.title: Generative AI at Work: From Exposure to Adoption across 35 European Countries
sources.gameUse: Strong EU-specific evidence that exposure does not equal adoption, and firm/worker/country institutions shape actual AI use
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [NEW].

sources.title: Payrolls to Prompts: Firm-Level Evidence on the Substitution of Labor for AI
sources.gameUse: Measures actual spending substitution rather than only occupational exposure; good for labor-market event cards

sources.title: Generative AI and the Reorganization of Labor Demand
sources.authors: Fangyan Wang, Zaiyan Wei, Yang Wang
sources.gameUse: Jobs don't just disappear; firms reallocate and redesign tasks — exactly the nuance worldview presets should expose
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [BALANCE].

sources.title: A meta-analysis of the effect of generative AI on productivity and learning in programming
sources.gameUse: Evidence-based antidote to both AI-changes-nothing and AI-replaces-programmers-now; useful for education/upskilling mechanics
sources.note: PRIORITY-VERIFY-ARXIV-2026: gap audit flags 2026-dated arXiv IDs (2602.xxxxx–2606.xxxxx) as the likeliest entries to be wrong; verify before citing. gap-audit tag: [BALANCE].

sources.gameUse: Public-legitimacy, censorship, and whose-values-count mechanics, comparing U.S. and European political cultures

sources.title: Public Opinion and The Rise of Digital Minds: Perceived Risk, Trust, and Regulation Support
sources.gameUse: Regulation-support mechanics; ties trust/risk perception to policy appetite

sources.title: Military AI Needs Technically-Informed Regulation to Safeguard AI Research and its Applications
sources.gameUse: Starter source for a dedicated autonomous-weapons / military-AI category

sources.gameUse: Useful for delivery-format decisions; shows game-based learning can remain effective outside the classroom

sources.authors: current and former OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees

sources.title: Diplomacy and Domestic Politics: The Logic of Two-Level Games
sources.gameUse: treaty ratification requires BOTH governments' domestic standing (political capital) at signing; the win-set logic behind the ratification window mechanic
